seoTitle: Hilltown vs Hincks — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hilltown and Hincks. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hilltown and Hincks.
heading: Hilltown vs Hincks — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hilltown, South Australia and Hincks, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hincks has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Hilltown (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hilltown or Hincks safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hilltown has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Hincks scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hilltown scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Hincks scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hilltown or Hincks?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hincks has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
